jobs.description:
We are seeking an experienced Operations Planning Leader to drive strategic network forecasting and inventory optimization in our Special Fulfillment team, with a focus on Express Mart and Amazon Now services. This role will be based at Tokyo and will be instrumental in transforming our delivery logistics through innovative planning strategies and data-driven decision-making.

In this position, you will lead the development of comprehensive inbound plans from Distribution Centers (DC) or Fulfillment Centers (FC) to Express Mart or Amazon Now sites, optimizing resource allocation across our expanding delivery network. You'll work at the intersection of strategic planning and operational excellence, creating forecasting models that balance fullness targets, cost efficiency and other metrics.

As a key member of our Supply Chain Management team, you'll collaborate with cross-functional partners including Business, Supply Chain Optimization Technologies (SCOT), Transport Services, and Finance to align operational strategies across Japan.

This role requires a strategic thinker who can independently lead complex planning operations, translate data insights into actionable plans, and maintain flexibility to adapt strategies based on evolving business needs. You'll be responsible for driving accuracy in inbound planning (Plan vs. Actual variance minimization), maintaining optimal inventory levels, and ensuring cost adherence to operational plansРђћall while supporting the next generation of customer delivery experiences.

If you thrive in fast-paced, innovative environments and are passionate about revolutionizing delivery networks through analytical rigor and collaborative problem-solving, this is your opportunity to make a significant impact on Japan's logistics landscape.

Key job responsibilities
Strategic Planning & Forecasting
- Develop 13-week rolling inbound plans from DC/FC for Express Mart and Amazon Now operations across Japan
- Create network forecasts optimizing inventory placement, Fullness target, cost efficiency and other metrics
- Lead S&OP processes for Express Mart and Amazon Now operations, aligning demand forecasts with supply capabilities

Operational Excellence
- Drive Plan vs. Actual variance minimization through continuous monitoring and adjustment
- Analyze operational metrics including site fullness, inventory health (DoC, WoC), transfer efficiency, and cost adherence
- Conduct deep-dive analyses to identify root causes and develop data-driven solutions

Cross-Functional Collaboration
- Partner with Business, SCOT, AMZL, and Finance teams to align planning strategies

Network Expansion & Innovation
- Support regional network expansion and new site launches
- Lead planning initiatives for emerging services

Process Improvement
- Identify and implement process improvements enhancing planning accuracy and operational efficiency
- Lead complex cross-functional projects with accountability for deliverables and timelines

About the team
We are the Special Fulfillment team in Supply Chain Management driving innovation in Japan's delivery logistics. Based at Tokyo), our collaborative group focuses on optimizing Express Mart, Amazon Now and Cold Chain operations across Japan. We partner closely with Business, SCOT, AMZL and Finance to revolutionize network planning and inventory optimization.

Our team thrives on data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement, balancing operational efficiency with customer experience. We're committed to expanding regional delivery capabilities, including emerging cold chain logistics, while maintaining the highest standards of planning accuracy and cost efficiency. Join us in shaping the future of Japan's fulfillment network.

jobs.description:
We are looking for detail-oriented, organized, and responsible individuals who are eager to learn how to apply their causal inference and/or structural econometrics skillsets to solve real world problems. The intern will work in the area of Economics Intelligence in Amazon Returns and Recommerce Technology and Innovation and develop new, data-driven solutions to support the most critical components of this rapidly scaling team.

Our PhD Economist Internship Program offers hands-on experience in applied economics, supported by mentorship, structured feedback, and professional development. Interns work on real business and research problems, building skills that prepare them for full-time economist roles at Amazon and beyond. You will learn how to build data sets and perform applied econometric analysis collaborating with economists, scientists, and product managers. These skills will translate well into writing applied chapters in your dissertation and provide you with work experience that may help you with placement.

These are full-time positions at 40 hours per week, with compensation being awarded on an hourly basis.

About the team

jobs.description:
Amazon has co-founded and signed The Climate Pledge, a commitment to reach net zero carbon by 2040. As a team, we leverage GenAI, sensors, smart home devices, cloud services, material science, and Alexa to build products that have a meaningful impact for customers and the climate.

In alignment with this bold corporate goal, the Amazon Devices & Services organization is looking for a passionate, talented, and inventive Senior Applied Scientist to help build revolutionary products with potential for major societal impact. Great candidates for this position will have expertise in the areas of agentic AI applications, deep learning, time series analysis, LLMs, and multimodal systems. This includes experience designing autonomous AI agents that can reason, plan, and execute multi-step tasks, building tool-augmented LLM systems with access to external APIs and data sources, implementing multi-agent orchestration, and developing RAG architectures that combine LLMs with domain-specific knowledge bases. You will strive for simplicity and creativity, demonstrating high judgment backed by statistical proof.


Key job responsibilities
As a Senior Applied Scientist on the Energy Science team, you'll design and deploy agentic AI systems that autonomously analyze data, plan solutions, and execute recommendations. You'll build multi-agent architectures where specialized AI agents coordinate to solve complex optimization problems, and develop tool-augmented LLM applications that integrate with external data sources and APIs to deliver context-aware insights.

Your work involves creating multimodal AI systems that synthesize diverse data streams, while implementing RAG pipelines that ground large language models in domain-specific knowledge bases. You'll apply advanced machine learning and deep learning techniques to time series analysis, forecasting, and pattern recognition.

Beyond technical innovation, you'll drive end-to-end product development from research through production deployment, collaborating with cross-functional teams to translate AI capabilities into customer experiences. You'll establish rigorous experimentation frameworks to validate model performance and measure business impact, building AI-driven products with potential for major societal impact.

сѓбсЃъсѓЙсЃ│сѓИсЃБсЃЉсЃ│сЂ«сЃЋсЃФсЃЋсѓБсЃФсЃАсЃ│сЃѕсѓ╗сЃ│сѓ┐сЃ╝№╝ѕуЅЕТхЂТІауѓ╣№╝ЅсЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІсђЂсЃАсЃ│сЃєсЃісЃ│сѓ╣сЃЂсЃ╝сЃаТЦГтІЎсЂ«сЃъсѓисЃ│сѓГсЃ╝сЃЉсЃ╝сѓњТІЁтйЊсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЪсЂасЂЇсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ

сЃАсЃ│сЃєсЃісЃ│сѓ╣сЃЂсЃ╝сЃасЂ«СИ╗сЂфТЦГтІЎсЂ»С╗ЦСИІсЂ«жђџсѓісЂДсЂЎсђѓ
